claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 14 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 14 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin the eligibility assessment.

**First, I need to know about the child you're claiming for:**

How old is the child?

- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

Please let me know which age category applies to your child.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Oh hi, thanks for helping me with this. I'm asking about my child Alex - they're 14 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thanks for that information! Since Alex is 14 years old, they fall into the **under 16** category, which is a good start for eligibility.

Let me continue with the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

Is Alex working **less than 24 hours per week** (or not in paid work at all)?

This is typically straightforward for a 14-year-old, as most children this age aren't in regular employment. But please confirm - is Alex either:
- Not working, OR
- Working fewer than 24 hours per week?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't work at all - they're just in school like any other 14-year-old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, that's exactly what I expected! Since Alex is in school and not working, let me continue to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

Is Alex **NOT receiving any benefits in their own right**, such as:
- Employment and Support Allowance (ESA)
- Universal Credit

To clarify, this is about benefits that Alex themselves would be claiming, not any benefits you as a parent might receive. For most 14-year-olds, this wouldn't apply.

Is that correct - Alex is not receiving any such benefits in their own name?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right, Alex doesn't get any benefits in their own name. They're just a regular kid.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, that's what I'd expect for a 14-year-old. Let's move on to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

Is Alex currently in an apprenticeship?

Since Alex is 14 and in school, I imagine the answer is no, but I just need to confirm. 

- **Not in an apprenticeship** (this would include Alex, as a regular school student)
- In a **Foundation Apprenticeship in Wales** (this is an approved type)
- In another type of apprenticeship (these can disqualify eligibility)

Is Alex simply in school and **not** doing any apprenticeship?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't doing an apprenticeship - just regular school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, thanks for confirming. Let's continue with the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

Now I need to check your residency status. You can qualify through **any one** of the following:

1. **Normally live in the UK** - This is the most common situation
2. **Settled status** under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. **Pre-settled status** with additional conditions (e.g., working, studying, or seeking work)
4. **Crown servant** living abroad

Do any of these apply to you? For most people, it's simply that they **normally live in the UK**. Is that your situation?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Actually, I don't live in the UK at the moment - I'm living abroad.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. Since you're living abroad, I need to check if you might still qualify through one of the special circumstances.

**Do any of these apply to you?**

1. **Crown servant living abroad** - This includes roles like:
   - Working for the UK government overseas (e.g., diplomatic service, armed forces)
   - Certain other qualifying government positions abroad

2. **Living in a qualifying country** with specific arrangements

Are you living abroad because you're a **Crown servant** (working for the UK government), or is it for other reasons (such as personal choice, private employment abroad, etc.)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not a Crown servant - I'm just living abroad for personal reasons.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying. Unfortunately, this affects your eligibility for Child Benefit.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'm sorry, but based on the information you've provided, **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

---

## Assessment Result: **NOT ELIGIBLE**

### Summary for Alex:
Although Alex meets all the requirements on the child's side:
- ✅ Under 16 years old
- ✅ Not working
- ✅ Not receiving benefits in their own right
- ✅ Not in a disqualifying apprenticeship

The issue is with the **residency requirement**:
- ❌ You do not normally live in the UK
- ❌ You are not a Crown servant or in another qualifying category

---

### What This Means:
Child Benefit is a UK benefit that requires the claimant (parent/guardian) to normally live in the UK, unless they qualify through special circumstances like being a Crown servant abroad or having settled/pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme.

### If Your Circumstances Change:
If you return to live in the UK in the future, you may be able to claim Child Benefit at that time. You would need to be "normally resident" in the UK, which generally means it's your main home and you intend to stay.

### Further Information:
You can find more details on the official Gov.uk website: https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/eligibility

---

Is there anything else you'd like me to clarify about this decision?

reasonings: Alex is 14 years old (under 16), not working, not receiving benefits in their own right, and not in an apprenticeship - all of which would normally support eligibility. However, the claimant (parent) does not meet the UK residency requirements. They are living abroad for personal reasons and are not a Crown servant or in any other qualifying category (such as having settled/pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme). UK Child Benefit requires the claimant to normally live in the UK or meet specific exemptions, which are not met in this case.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex because they do not meet the UK residency requirements. While Alex meets all the child-related criteria (under 16, not working, no benefits, not in apprenticeship), the claimant must normally live in the UK or qualify through special circumstances such as being a Crown servant or having settled/pre-settled status. Living abroad for personal reasons does not meet these requirements.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.reason: Does not meet UK residency requirements
response.guidance: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, Crown servant, or living in certain countries). If you have pre-settled status, you may need to be working, studying, or have sufficient resources.

UK Child Benefit requires the claimant to normally live in the UK or meet specific exemptions, which are not met in this case.

Living abroad for personal reasons does not meet these requirements.
